description: A community-published opinionated path with structured applicability conditions and optional verification requirements. v0.2.0 replaces the free-text conditions/preconditions/caveats fields with structured ThCondition combinator trees and a typed caveat list; requiredVerifications upgrades from a verification-kind array to an array of LensProperty so consumers can check specific theorems, not just kinds. Narrative prose moves to `annotations` / `caveatsText`.

main.description: A community endorses a lens path under structured conditions. Consumers evaluate the condition tree against their invocation context to decide applicability; the caveats list gives structured failure modes to match on.

conditions.description: Structured applicability predicate in postfix-operator form: each item is either an atomic predicate over the invocation context or a combinator (and/or/not) that pops operands from the running stack. An empty array means 'always applies'. See dev.idiolect.theory.condition.

preconditions.description: Additional structured assumptions the consumer must verify before adopting (same combinator shape as `conditions`). Empty array means no explicit preconditions.

lensPath.description: Ordered sequence of lenses to compose. Length 1 means a single recommended lens.

annotations.description: Why this path, how to use it, known strengths. Narrative; not consumed by machine matchers.

requiredVerifications.description: Verification properties this recommendation assumes are in place. v0.2.0 upgrade: each entry is a specific LensProperty, not just a verification kind, so consumers can check which roundtrip domain / theorem / standard has been established.

caveats.description: Structured known-failure-mode list. Consumers match on `mode` / `affects` / `severity` without substring search.

caveatsText.description: Narrative commentary on the caveats. Companion to the structured `caveats` list, not a replacement.

conditionSourceIs.description: Atomic predicate: the invocation's source schema is the given schema.

conditionTargetIs.description: Atomic predicate: the invocation's target schema is the given schema.

conditionPurposeSubsumedBy.description: Atomic predicate: the invocation's purpose.action is subsumed by the given purpose.action under the community vocabulary. Reuses the same subsumption hierarchy that governs data-use permissions.

conditionDataHas.description: Atomic predicate: the invocation's data has the named property.

conditionAnd.description: Combinator: conjoin the top two predicates on the evaluation stack.

conditionOr.description: Combinator: disjoin the top two predicates on the evaluation stack.

conditionNot.description: Combinator: negate the top predicate on the evaluation stack.
